saw_palmetto_fronds.description: Saw palmetto! Its tough fan leaves survive wildfires, and its berries feed birds and bears.

scrub_hickory_nuts.description: Scrub hickory nuts! They only grow in Florida's sandy scrub. Mice bury them and forget, planting new trees.

sand_pine_cones.description: Sand pine cones! They need wildfire to pop open and release their seeds. Fire keeps the scrub alive.

florida_rosemary_cuttings.description: Florida rosemary! A pioneer plant. It's one of the first to return after a scrub fire.

rusty_lyonia_flowers.description: Rusty lyonia, also called staggerbush. Its spring blooms feed pollinators across the scrub.

chapman_oak_acorns.description: Chapman oak acorns! These stubby scrub oaks make a feast for scrub jays, who cache hundreds every fall.

flicker_feather.description: A flicker feather! Northern flickers flash bright yellow under their wings when they fly.

pip_map.description: Pip's map! A hand-drawn trail to the rarest scrub plants. The little mouse's gift.
